tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: A Stranger's Kindness Transforms a Senior's Life—and Uncovers an Olympian's Heart
stories.summary: A compassionate caretaker's spontaneous help to a struggling elderly man blossomed into a transformative friendship that restored his dignity and safety.

stories.headline: For Ten Years, One Man Rides 25 Kilometers to Feed Thirsty Birds in the Heat
stories.summary: Riyaz Pasha from Karnataka rides 25 kilometers regularly to bring water and grain to birds, inspiring his entire community to place water containers around their homes.

stories.headline: California Condor Soars Over Oregon for First Time in 120 Years
stories.summary: California condor B9 soared 380 miles over Oregon for four days, marking the first time in 120 years a free-flying condor returned to the state's skies.

stories.headline: A Bird Declared Extinct Takes Flight Again: 8 Crested Ibises Released in Japan
stories.summary: Eight crested ibises were released in Japan to join over 500 wild birds, bringing the species back from extinction after the last native Japanese ibis died in 2003.

stories.headline: Right Whales Welcome Their Most Calves Since 2009—New Hope for a Struggling Species
stories.summary: North Atlantic right whales welcomed 23 new calves in 2026, their strongest season since 2009, offering cautious hope for this critically endangered species.

stories.headline: After Six Years Without Care, a Woman Finds Relief at a Mobile Dental Clinic
stories.summary: Kathryn Finlay finally received dental care after a six-year wait, calling Dentaid's mobile clinic in Gorleston a 'godsend' in her journey back to health.

stories.headline: Detroit Zoo Releases Nearly 7,000 Endangered Toad Tadpoles Into Puerto Rico's Rainforests
stories.summary: Nearly 7,000 Puerto Rican crested toad tadpoles journeyed from Detroit to wetlands, advancing one of the world's most ambitious amphibian rescues.

stories.headline: Teen Gardeners Grow Plants for Food Bank, Learning That Giving Creates Bonds
stories.summary: Lake Pend Oreille High School students grew plants in their greenhouse and delivered them to the Bonner Community Food Bank, learning that giving to community strengthens bonds.

stories.headline: Tiny Parrot Seen Once in a Century Reappears in Indonesian Mountain Forest
stories.summary: The elusive Blue-fronted Lorikeet was photographed in April 2026 on Buru's highest peak, the first sighting in 12 years of Indonesia's rarest mountain parrot.

stories.headline: Malaysian Badminton Rising Star Upsets Higher-Ranked Dane in Super 1000 Debut
stories.summary: K. Letshanaa defeated an elite Danish opponent in her first Super 1000 tournament, proving her inspiring climb through the sport is just beginning.

stories.headline: Clean Power Flows 330 Miles: New York City Now Powered by Quebec's Dams
stories.summary: The Champlain Hudson Power Express, a 330-mile underground transmission line, now carries enough hydroelectric power from Quebec to serve about a million New York City homes.
